content.full_text:
From 
Subject : 
Marian Ash, Chief 
Political Reform Division 
Secretary of State's Office 
, ; 
n 
A . ./" C 
/' 
~)/~~ 
FAIR POUnCAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Date 
Incomplete Campaign Disclosure Statements 
May 2, 1984 
/ -
Your staff recently asked for FPPC guidance in determining 
the procedures to be followed when grossly incomplete campaign 
disclosure statements are filed. 
For example, your office has 
received statements which provide only summary page information 
(aggregate receipts and expenditures) but no itemized information 
regarding receipts and expenditures. 
The FPPC staff has determined that such incomplete 
statements should not be re3~cted or returned to the filer, but 
should be file-stamped and logged in in the same manner as other 
filings. 
In addition, such statements should not be considered 
nnon-filings
U for the purpose of assessing late fines. 
We suggest that a letter be sent to the candidate or 
committee.requesting that an amendment providing all of the 
required information be filed within 10 days, and indicating that 
if the required information is not filed within 10 days the 
statement will be referred to the FPPC Enforcement Division. 
Please send a copy of your letter and the incomplete filing to 
the Enforcement Division. 
In addition, if an amendment is not 
filed within 10 days, please notify the Enforcement Division. 
Such filing constitutes seriously inadequate campaign 
disclosure and our office will take into consideration several 
factors in determining what action will be taken. 
Such factors 
may include, among other factors, the volume and amount of 
receipts and expenditures not properly reported, whether it is 
the first time the candidate or committee has filed an incomplete 
statement, and the proximity of the filing deadline to the 
election. 
Thank you for bringing this to our attention. 
Please call 
me if you have any questions.

sections.question: Your staff recently asked for FPPC guidance in determining the procedures to be followed when grossly incomplete campaign disclosure statements are filed. For example, your office has received statements which provide only summary page information (aggregate receipts and expenditures) but no itemized information regarding receipts and expenditures.
sections.conclusion: The FPPC staff has determined that such incomplete statements should not be rejected or returned to the filer, but should be file-stamped and logged in the same manner as other filings. In addition, such statements should not be considered "non-filings" for the purpose of assessing late fines. A letter should be sent to the candidate or committee requesting that an amendment providing all required information be filed within 10 days, with notification that failure to comply will result in referral to the FPPC Enforcement Division.
sections.question_synthetic: What procedures should be followed when grossly incomplete campaign disclosure statements are filed, such as those containing only summary information without itemized details?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Incomplete campaign disclosure statements should be filed and logged normally without rejection, and the filer should be given 10 days to submit an amendment with complete information, with enforcement action threatened if the amendment is not filed. The FPPC will consider various factors including the volume of unreported receipts/expenditures, prior filing history, and election proximity when determining enforcement action.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear FPPC advice letter with identifiable question and conclusion sections. Document contains procedural guidance on campaign finance disclosure enforcement.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your staff recently asked for FPPC guidance in determining the procedures to be followed when grossly incomplete campaign disclosure statements are filed. For example, your office has received statements which provide only summary page information (aggregate receipts and expenditures) but no itemized information regarding receipts and expenditures.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC staff has determined that such incomplete statements should not be rejected or returned to the filer, but should be file-stamped and logged in the same manner as other filings. In addition, such statements should not be considered "non-filings" for the purpose of assessing late fines. A letter should be sent to the candidate or committee requesting that an amendment providing all required information be filed within 10 days, with notification that failure to comply will result in referral to the FPPC Enforcement Division.
embedding.first_500_words: From Subject : Marian Ash, Chief Political Reform Division Secretary of State's Office , ; n A . ./" C /' ~)/~~ FAIR POUnCAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Jeanne Pritchard Date Incomplete Campaign Disclosure Statements May 2, 1984 / - Your staff recently asked for FPPC guidance in determining the procedures to be followed when grossly incomplete campaign disclosure statements are filed. For example, your office has received statements which provide only summary page information (aggregate receipts and expenditures) but no itemized information regarding receipts and expenditures. The FPPC staff has determined that such incomplete statements should not be re3~cted or returned to the filer, but should be file-stamped and logged in in the same manner as other filings. In addition, such statements should not be considered nnon-filings U for the purpose of assessing late fines. We suggest that a letter be sent to the candidate or committee.requesting that an amendment providing all of the required information be filed within 10 days, and indicating that if the required information is not filed within 10 days the statement will be referred to the FPPC Enforcement Division. Please send a copy of your letter and the incomplete filing to the Enforcement Division. In addition, if an amendment is not filed within 10 days, please notify the Enforcement Division. Such filing constitutes seriously inadequate campaign disclosure and our office will take into consideration several factors in determining what action will be taken. Such factors may include, among other factors, the volume and amount of receipts and expenditures not properly reported, whether it is the first time the candidate or committee has filed an incomplete statement, and the proximity of the filing deadline to the election. Thank you for bringing this to our attention. Please call me if you have any questions.
embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the Secretary of State's office on handling grossly incomplete campaign disclosure statements by filing them normally, requesting amendments within 10 days, and referring non-compliant filers to the Enforcement Division.
